summary: Gruber catalogs undocumented new features in iPhone firmware 1.1.1 that weren't mentioned in Apple's official announcements. He highlights press-and-hold accented character input, Bonjour networking support, a JavaScript debug console in MobileSafari, and new text message notification sounds. The post serves as a crowd-sourced discovery list, inviting readers to report additional findings. He notes that some features also apply to the iPod Touch.
key_points: Press-and-hold on keyboard letters now reveals accented character variants via a pop-up menu, a significant usability improvement for multilingual typing
Bonjour/.local networking finally works on iPhone, fixing what Gruber considered a curious omission from the original firmware
A JavaScript debug console for MobileSafari signals Apple taking web development on iPhone more seriously
New text message notification sounds are praised as superior to Mac OS X's sound design
money_quotes: After years of using Bonjour (née Rendezvous), 'it just seems weird to use IP addresses again.'
This was a curious omission from iPhone 1.0.
They're great sounds — iPhone has way better sounds than Mac OS X.
key_insight: Apple's iPhone 1.1.1 update quietly shipped several meaningful usability improvements — accented characters, Bonjour networking, developer tools — that went unmentioned in the official announcements.
